issue.body:
Decide whether to flip the reorganize-level default for `Page.reorganize_page(sidenote_max_height_ratio=...)` from `None` (legacy x-only) to e.g. `0.85` (more aggressive, bbox-height-aware).

Bbox-height pass already shipped; `detect_geometric_sidenotes(max_height_ratio=...)` rejects a margin cluster unless its median bbox height is `<= ratio * body_median_height`. Default `None` preserves legacy behaviour. Flipping the default needs evidence that `0.85`-style gating helps the corpus more than it hurts on real fixtures.

This is a tuning slice, not a coding slice: do a fixture sweep (and likely add fixtures) before changing the default.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (Glyph-size analysis - Default-flip decision).
Spec: docs/specs/03-reorganize-pipeline.md

## Acceptance
- [ ] Sweep all `tests/fixtures/layout_regression/` fixtures with `0.85` and record diffs vs `None`
- [ ] Add at least one fixture that demonstrates the gating helps (tall sidenote currently misclassified as body)
- [ ] Decision recorded in spec/ROADMAP: keep `None` default, or flip to `0.85`
- [ ] If default is flipped, all existing fixtures still pass or have reviewed/accepted diffs

nodes.body: Triage decision: approved + needs-spec. Spec child issue: #46. Run `/spec-from-issue 46` to produce the design spec.

issue.title: Sidenote detection: tune default for sidenote_max_height_ratio

issue.body:
TEST_COVERAGE_WORKPLAN Next Action #3: establish CI coverage reporting thresholds (target 88%, fail under 80%).

Mechanical: configure `pytest-cov` / coverage in `pyproject.toml` (or Makefile) so `make ci` fails when overall coverage drops below 80%, and add a soft-target report at 88%.

Existing coverage is 89.8% (CPU-only run, GPU modules excluded), so this is a forward-only gate.

Source: TEST_COVERAGE_WORKPLAN.md (Next Actions #3).

## Acceptance
- [ ] `make coverage` (or `make ci`) returns non-zero when overall coverage < 80%
- [ ] Coverage report surfaces an 88% soft-target indicator
- [ ] Documentation (README or CONTRIBUTING) reflects the thresholds
- [ ] CI passes at current 89.8% coverage

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #3

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#296

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/layout/registry.py:176`
- `pd_book_tools/layout/registry.py:179`
- `pd_book_tools/layout/registry.py:199`

When `get_detector(..., on_error="log_and_null")` catches a build failure, it caches a `NullDetector` under the same cache key used by later `on_error="raise"` calls.

Impact:

- A transient model download/OOM/build failure can make later fail-fast callers silently get no layout.
- Unknown-key fallbacks can likewise hide later valid configuration.

Remediation:

- Do not cache fallback detectors under normal success keys.
- Include the fallback policy in the cache key, or make `on_error="raise"` bypass cached fallbacks.
- Add regression tests for failure-then-raise and unknown-key-then-register flows.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: layout fallback cache poisoning
